title: Anchor init_if_needed account lacks a reinitialization guard

params.passDetail: Account with init_if_needed has an explicit reinitialization guard.
params.failAbsentDetail: Account uses #[account(init_if_needed)] without checking whether it is already initialized. A malicious caller can invoke the instruction a second time to overwrite the account's data — a full state-wipe with no on-chain recourse.
params.failDynamicDetail: Reinitialization guard is present but uses a dynamic expression; cannot confirm it covers all paths statically.

params.description: Calls the instruction twice on the same account; second call must fail with AlreadyInitialized.

provenance.note: init_if_needed re-runs the instruction body on every call including re-invocations against already-initialized accounts. Without an explicit guard (require! or equivalent), a second call can overwrite the account state. This requires Rust AST inspection (tree-sitter-rust) and is deferred to Phase 2.5. Expected to route to DRAFT in Phase 2.
